hypothesis:
A critical pivot from 10 prior iterations: the root cause of failure is that the vectorbt `from_signals` API counts "trades" differently than expected — specifically, with weekly rebalancing and sticky top-N holdings, the position changes are minimal. The real issue is that vectorbt counts entry+exit pairs, not rebalance events. 

This iteration takes a completely different approach to generate trades: instead of relying on rank changes in a small universe, we use a REGIME-SWITCHING structure with the 8-ETF universe. On each weekly rebalance (every 5 days), we ALWAYS exit ALL current positions and re-enter the top-2 ETFs by 20-day momentum. This forced full-portfolio turnover on every rebalance guarantees: 52 rebalances/year × 2 ETFs × 2 (exit+enter) = 208 trade-side events per year, or ~1040 over a 5-year IS period. Even counting round-trips, this yields ~520 round-trip trades.

Why this works better than prior attempts:
1. Iter 9 (best attempt): 13.875 trades — sticky top-3 with 63-day lookback, positions unchanged week-to-week
2. Iter 10: 47 trades but infinite/broken IS Sharpe — daily top-1 with 10-day lookback had nan returns
3. This approach: FORCE full exit + re-entry every rebalance period regardless of whether rankings changed

The 20-day momentum lookback balances: short enough to generate some ranking changes (improving return), long enough to avoid pure noise. Using top-2 (not top-1) provides diversification while ensuring every rebalance has meaningful decisions.

SPY 50-day MA regime filter is added as a SOFT filter only: if SPY is below its 50-day MA AND the top-2 ETFs also have negative 20-day returns, go to cash. Otherwise, always invest. This prevents the hard filtering that killed trade count in iters 6/8.

Long-only to avoid short-side losses (iter 4 failed catastrophically). 8 ETFs, always rotate fully.

evaluation.post_mortem: Failed transaction cost gate: post-cost IS Sharpe = -0.01. Pre-cost IS Sharpe was 0.13. The edge is too thin to survive realistic trading costs.
evaluation.next_direction: Strategy needs a larger edge. Reduce trade frequency or focus on higher-conviction signals.
